primary_outcomes.measure: Percentage of Participants With at Least One Adverse Event Reported Spontaneously by the Participant/or Caregiver (Including Parent/Legal Guardian) or Observed by the Investigator During the Study
primary_outcomes.description:
An Adverse Event (AE) is any untoward medical occurrence in a patient or clinical investigation participant administered a pharmaceutical product, which does not necessarily have a causal relationship with this treatment. An AE could therefore be any unfavorable and unintended sign, symptom, or disease temporally associated with the use of a medicinal (investigational) product, whether or not related to the medicinal (investigational) product.

26 adverse events are reported splitting into at least 19 occurrences of individual pre-treatment emergent adverse events and 7 treatment emergent adverse events (TEAEs).
primary_outcomes.timeFrame: From Screening Period (Day -7 to Day -1) up to the End-of-Study Period (up to Day 37)

primary_outcomes.measure: Percentage of Participants That Withdrew Due to Adverse Events During the Study
primary_outcomes.description: An Adverse Event (AE) is any untoward medical occurrence in a patient or clinical investigation participant administered a pharmaceutical product, which does not necessarily have a causal relationship with this treatment and led to the withdrawal of the participants from the study. An AE could therefore be any unfavorable and unintended sign, symptom, or disease temporally associated with the use of a medicinal (investigational) product, whether or not related to the medicinal (investigational) product.
primary_outcomes.timeFrame: From Screening Period (Day -7 to Day -1) up to the End-of-Study Period (up to Day 37)
